label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001 isn't. So Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So we need to find out what today's date is. If she was born in 2001, then her 16th birthday would be in 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. Now, the question is asking what the date was 10 days ago from today. So subtracting 10 days from February 28, 2017. Let me think. Let's count backwards. February 28 minus 1 day is 02/27, then 02/26... down to 02/18. Wait, 28 minus 10 is 18. But wait, we need to make sure that February in 2017 has 28 days. Since 2017 isn't a leap year (2016 was a leap year, then 2020), so yes, February 2017 has 28 days. So 10 days before February 28, 2017 is February 18, 2017. So the answer should be 02/18/2017, which is option B. Wait, but let me double-check. Let me count each day. If today is the 28th, then 1 day before is 27th, 2 days is 26th, 3 days 25th, 4 days 24th, 5
